quiz.title: AP Calculus AB Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Applications of Derivatives — Motion, Rates, and Linear Approximation (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: The position of a particle moving along a line is given by $s(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 + 4t - 1$, where $s$ is in meters and $t$ is in seconds. Find the velocity of the particle at $t = 3$ seconds.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the curve $f(x) = x^3 - 2x$ at the point where $x = -1$.
The radius of a circle is increasing at a rate of 0.5 cm/s. How fast is the area of the circle increasing when the radius is 8 cm? (Area $A = \pi r^2$)
questions.explanation: Area $A = \pi r^2$, so $ rac{dA}{dt} = 2\pi r  rac{dr}{dt}$. At $r = 8$, $ rac{dr}{dt} = 0.5$: $ rac{dA}{dt} = 2\pi(8)(0.5) = 8\pi$ cm²/s.

questions.question: The profit (in dollars) from selling $x$ units of a product is given by $P(x) = -2x^2 + 200x - 3000$. Find the marginal profit when $x = 30$.
questions.explanation: Marginal profit $P'(x) = -4x + 200$. At $x = 30$: $P'(30) = -4(30) + 200 = -120 + 200 = 80$ dollars per unit.

questions.question: A car's distance from a stop sign (in feet) is given by $d(t) = 10t + 5t^2$, where $t$ is in seconds after passing the sign. What is the car's velocity at $t = 4$ seconds?
Find the equation of the tangent line to $f(x) = \frac{1}{x}$ at the point $(2, 0.5)$. Use it to estimate the value of $\frac{1}{2.1}$.
questions.explanation: $f'(x) = -\frac{1}{x^2}$, slope at $x = 2$ is $-\frac{1}{4} = -0.25$. Tangent line: $y - 0.5 = -0.25(x - 2)$ or $y = -0.25x + 1$. Estimate: $\frac{1}{2.1} \approx -0.25(2.1) + 1 = -0.525 + 1 = 0.475$. (Actual value is approximately 0.4762).

questions.question: Using the position function $s(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 + 4t - 1$, find the acceleration of the particle at $t = 2$ seconds.
The height of a growing plant (in cm) is modeled by $H(t) = 5 + 2\ln(t + 1)$, where $t$ is in weeks. Find the instantaneous growth rate at $t = 4$ weeks.
The volume of a sphere is increasing at a rate of $36\pi$ cm³/s. At what rate is the radius increasing when the radius is 3 cm? (Volume $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$)
questions.explanation: $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, so $ rac{dV}{dt} = 4\pi r^2  rac{dr}{dt}$. Given $ rac{dV}{dt} = 36\pi$ and $r = 3$: $36\pi = 4\pi(9) rac{dr}{dt} = 36\pi rac{dr}{dt}$. Thus $ rac{dr}{dt} = 1$ cm/s.

questions.question: If a function $f$ has a derivative $f'(x) > 0$ for all $x$ in the interval $(a, b)$, what does this tell you about the behavior of $f$ on that interval?
options: The function is strictly decreasing on the interval
The function is strictly increasing on the interval
The function has a maximum on the interval
The function is constant on the interval
questions.explanation: When $f'(x) > 0$ on an interval $(a, b)$, the function $f$ is strictly increasing on that interval. For any $x_1 < x_2$ in the interval, $f(x_1) < f(x_2)$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered applications of derivatives.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand most derivative applications.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on setting up problems correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review basic differentiation rules and their applications.
